meta.title: Sála · Restaurant · Prague · Vinohrady
meta.description: Sála — a small neighbourhood restaurant on Vinohradská. Modern Czech cooking with locally sourced ingredients, natural wine, tasting menu six nights a week. Reservations open.

hero.title: Sála
hero.sub: A small neighbourhood restaurant in Vinohrady. Modern Czech cooking with locally sourced ingredients, natural wine and an open kitchen. Forty seats, six evenings a week.

tonight.title: Roasted celeriac with brown butter, fermented gooseberry & summer herbs
tonight.desc: A vegetable course we've been working on since the autumn. Whole celeriac roasted in salt for four hours, glazed with our brown butter (we make a fresh batch every Tuesday), and finished with a gooseberry ferment from the August harvest. Served with chervil and mountain savory dried in our kitchen.
tonight.price: À la carte · 320 Kč · In the tasting · course 4 of 7

manifesto.title: We cook with what's local — and what the season brings
manifesto.p1: Sála is a small restaurant in a 1908 dining hall. The tin ceiling, tile floor and wide Vinohradská windows are original. We added a wood-burning grill, an open kitchen and a desire to cook modern Czech food from what grows nearby.
manifesto.p2: Almost everything on the plate comes from within forty kilometres. Vegetables from the Čermák family in Lhota, old-breed pork and lamb from Statek u Cibulky, cheese from Ovocný Trh, eggs from Kostelec.
manifesto.p3: One tasting menu and a short à la carte — both change every two weeks. Wine is natural, mostly Moravian and Austrian. Closed Sundays and Mondays so the whole team gets two days off in a row.
manifesto.p4: Come hungry and stay a while. We're happy to top up your wine.

tasting.title:
Seven courses,
one menu, ninety minutes
tasting.lede: Hover a course to see the plate. The menu changes every other Tuesday. Vegetarian and gluten-free versions available — just let us know when you book.

c1.name: Cold-smoked trout with dill oil and pickled rye
c1.detail: Trout from Český Krumlov, smoked over apple wood. Cucumber from our kitchen garden, brined in last year's whey. Dill flowers.

c2.name: Twelve-hour broth with summer tomatoes
c2.detail: Aged beef bones, charred onion and our smoked tomato preserve from August. Finished tableside with marigold oil.

c3.name: Roasted celeriac, brown butter and fermented gooseberry
c3.detail: Celeriac roasted in salt for four hours and glazed with our brown butter. Gooseberry ferment from August. Served on a stone, finished with mountain savory.

c4.name: Sourdough bread course with cultured butter and lardo
c4.detail: We've kept this sourdough alive in our kitchen since November 2021. Pulled hot from the wood oven, served with cultured butter from Vysočina and six-month lardo.

c5.name: Wild duck with fermented blueberry and charred cabbage
c5.detail: Bohemian wild duck, dry-aged on the bone for ten days, glazed with last summer's blueberries. Charred pointed cabbage from the Čermáks. Pan jus poured at the table.

c6.name: Aged cheeses from Ovocný Trh
c6.detail: Three Czech cheeses, aged eight, twelve and eighteen months in a cellar two streets away. Served with walnut-leaf bread and plum mostarda from our September pickle.

c7.name: Buckwheat sponge cake with caramelised honey and sour cream
c7.detail: A warm cake of toasted buckwheat. Sour cream from Kostelec, glazed with honey from the orchard at Modletice — caramelised and cooled twice.

wine.kicker: The cellar · 180+ Czech, Moravian and Austrian growers
wine.title:
Living wine,
from the people who grow it
wine.lede: A short list of mostly natural wines. Most are available by the glass. Bringing your own bottle? Corkage is 350 Kč and goes to the kitchen tip jar.

w1.name: Pálava 2022
w1.notes: Apricot skin, dried marigold, gentle salt. A kitchen wine — works with anything off the grill.

w2.name: Veltlín nature 2021
w2.notes: Six days on skins, unfiltered. Quince, white tea and a clean bitter finish — the wine for the celeriac course.

w3.name: Modrý Portugal Pet-Nat 2023
w3.notes: Bottled half-fermented, cold-aged on the lees. Wild raspberry, sourdough and a touch of salt. Drinks like a beer.

w4.name: Frankovka 1976 vines · 2018
w4.notes: Fifty-year-old vines, eight months in old oak. Smoke, blackberry leaf, iron — pours beautifully with the duck or the cheese.

press.title: What people have written
press.q1: "The most quietly confident new restaurant in the city. Anna Hofmann is cooking food that tastes specifically of her — and of Vinohrady, in this exact week."
press.q2: "Forty seats, one tasting menu, an open kitchen — Sála has the kind of conviction Prague has been waiting for."
press.q3: "Wine list of the year. The Moravian-Austrian focus is the smartest editing in the country."

chef.title: Anna Hofmann cooks here every night
chef.p1: Anna was born in Olomouc and trained in Vienna. After two years at Frantzén in Stockholm and a year at Maaemo in Oslo, she returned to Prague in 2021 to open Sála.
chef.p2: Six nights a week she works the pass and plates almost every dish herself. She writes the menu two weeks at a time, around what the season brings.

private.title: A separate room, one long table for twelve
private.text: Below the dining room sits a vaulted brick cellar with one oak table for twelve. You get your own wine list, printed menus with your guests' names and Anna at the door. Birthdays, business dinners and the occasional small wedding.
private.l2: Tasting menu only · wine pairing optional · printed menus for every guest
private.l4: From 2 600 Kč per guest · drinks billed separately

visit.title:
Open six nights a week,
in the same room since 2021
visit.lede: Two minutes' walk from Náměstí Míru. We're on the corner of Vinohradská and Šafaříkova — look for the deep-green door and the tin ceiling visible through the window.

p.title: Reservations open · this week
p.text: Book online in under a minute. We hold a few seats at the bar for walk-ins from 17:30 — first come, first served.

meta.title: Menu · Sála restaurant · Prague
meta.description: The full Sála menu — tasting, à la carte, sides and desserts. Updated every two weeks.

h.title:
A small kitchen,
cooking by the season
h.lede: Two ways to eat at Sála — the seven-course tasting written around what's in season, or four à-la-carte chapters with a couple of dishes in each. Whatever you choose, we cook it ourselves; the chef is in the kitchen every night.

b1.name: Sourdough & cultured butter
b1.desc: Our wood-oven sourdough, kept alive in this kitchen since 2021. Cultured butter from Vysočina, smoked Maldon salt. veg

b2.name: Cold-smoked trout, pickled rye
b2.desc: Trout from Český Krumlov, dill oil, sour cream from Kostelec. Toasted pickled rye.

b3.name: Beef tartare, charred onion, capers
b3.desc: Aged ribcap from Statek u Cibulky, hand-cut. Charred onion broth, salted capers, raw yolk.

f1.name: Roasted celeriac, brown butter, fermented gooseberry
f1.desc: Whole celeriac roasted in salt for four hours, glazed with brown butter (we make it fresh every Tuesday). veg

f2.name: Beetroot tartare, black walnut
f2.desc: Heritage beets from the Čermáks, smoked over hay. Pickled black walnut, horseradish cream. veggf

f3.name: Wood-fired hispi cabbage, caraway, brown butter
f3.desc: Pointed cabbage charred over beech, brown butter sauce, toasted caraway. veg

f4.name: Pearl barley with aged cheese and wild herbs
f4.desc: Risotto-style pearl barley with two-year-old cheese from Ovocný Trh and herbs from the kitchen window. veg

r1.name: Wild duck, fermented blueberry, charred cabbage
r1.desc: Bohemian wild duck, dry-aged on the bone for ten days. Charred cabbage, pan jus poured at the table.

r2.name: Old-breed pork shoulder, caraway, juniper
r2.desc: Přeštice pork from Statek u Cibulky, slow-cooked over beech for nine hours. Pickled juniper, caraway jus.

r3.name: Aged ribeye, charred onion, bone marrow
r3.desc: 42-day-aged Šumava beef, charred over fire. Charred onion sauce, marrow on toast. share for 2

r4.name: Whole baked turbot, brown butter, sea aster
r4.desc: Day-boat turbot from Brittany, baked on the bone. Brown butter, sea aster, salted lemon. share for 2

s1.name: Buckwheat sponge, caramelised honey, sour cream
s1.desc: A warm cake of toasted buckwheat. Sour cream from Kostelec, twice-burnt honey from Modletice. veg

s2.name: Plum & oat crumble
s2.desc: Plums from our September pickle, oat crumble, smoked vanilla cream. veg

s3.name: Sour-cream ice cream, blackcurrant, malt
s3.desc: Sour-cream ice cream churned daily. Pickled blackcurrant, toasted malt crumb. veggf

s4.name: Cheese plate from Ovocný Trh
s4.desc: Three Czech cheeses, aged 8, 12 and 18 months. Walnut-leaf bread, plum mostarda. veg

menu.key1: Allergens · please tell us when you book

meta.title: Reservations · Sála restaurant
meta.description: Book a table at Sála — Vinohradská 27, Prague. Tasting menu and à la carte. Open Tue–Sat.

resv.strip: Tables released 14 days ahead · Tuesday — Saturday

h.title:
Three steps,
under a minute
h.lede: We hold a few seats at the bar for walk-ins, but most evenings the dining room books out two weeks ahead. Choose a date, time and how many of you, and we'll confirm by email.

st1.title: When would you like to come?
st1.text: Pick a date in the next 14 days. Sittings begin at 17:30, last seating at 21:30 (22:00 on Saturday).

st2.title: How many of you?
st2.text: Up to 6 guests online. For 7+ please email us about the Cellar Room — we'll set the long table for you.

st3.title: A few last details
st3.text: We use these only to confirm your booking and reach you if anything changes. Allergies and special requests are welcome — we cook around them.
st3.name: Full name
st3.notesPh: e.g. one of us is vegetarian, one is allergic to celeriac, it's our anniversary

st4.title: See you soon at Sála
st4.text: A confirmation is on its way to your inbox. We hold tables for 15 minutes after the booking time — if you're running late, just give us a quick call and we'll keep your seat.

sum.title: Your booking · Sála
sum.text: A small kitchen, forty seats, one tasting menu and a short à la carte. We'll write back within 24 hours.
sum.note:
"We're happy to top up your wine."
— Anna
